rulings.Case Title: GEORGE LUCAS,JR et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Appoint Dorothy Lewis As Successor-In-Interest To, And Substitute Successor-In-Interest For, Deceased Plaintiff And For Leave To File First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: ON CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, APRIL 22, 2010 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30AM, LINE 1. Plaintiff George Lucas¿ motion to appoint Dorothy Lewis as successor-in-interest to, and substitute successor-in-interest for, deceased plaintiff and for leave to file first amended complaint is granted in part and denied in part. Plaintiff¿s motion is granted as to appointing Dorothy Lewis as successor-in-interest for deceased plaintiff. Plaintiff¿s motion for leave to amend the complaint to name decedent¿s natural children, George Lucas, III, Edward Lucas and Patricia Maier, as decedent¿s legal heirs is granted. Plaintiff¿s motion for leave to amend the complaint to add the prospective adoptees, James Mark, Jr., Tracie Robertson and Leslie Villanueva, as decedent¿s legal heirs is denied, as there was no allegation that the adoption by decedent of these three people was finalized. Defendant Ray¿s General Hardware¿s request for sanctions is denied, as the request failed to comply with C.C.P. § 128.7. = (220/HEK)
